title: SPARTA Arbitrage Data Contract v1
research_objective: Define exactly what data WOULD be required to later test the five Arbitrage Research Protocol v1 categories. This contract does not fetch data, does not connect to any exchange, does not establish any data feed, and does not authorize trading. It is the written input spec that any future, separately-authorized data-acquisition bundle (Protocol P2) must satisfy.

supported_arbitrage_categories.label: A. Cross-exchange spot arbitrage
data_needed: Synchronized bid/ask quotes (L1, optionally L2) on the SAME UTC clock for both venues, with documented per-venue clock skew.
Venue fee schedules: maker, taker, and any volume-tier overrides.
Spread series at quote time (best_bid, best_ask, mid, microprice if available).
Depth at the size we intend to test (cumulative notional / quantity available at the touch and at +1 to +5 ticks).
Withdrawal and deposit constraints: minimum amounts, daily caps, fees, network tokens accepted.
Transfer delay assumptions: conservative (slow-day) latency between venues, per asset and per network.
Asset/network compatibility: whether the asset on Venue A can settle on the same network as Venue B (e.g., ERC-20 vs. native).
category_specific_validity: A row is invalid if either venue's quote is missing on the aligned timestamp.
A row is invalid if depth at the test size is unavailable on either venue at that timestamp.
A row is invalid if the asset/network is not transferable between the two venues.

supported_arbitrage_categories.label: B. Spot-perp basis / funding arbitrage
data_needed: Spot bid/ask history aligned with the perp's mark/index timestamps.
Perp bid/ask history (mark price, index price, and last-traded price).
Funding-rate history per venue (rate, timestamp, settlement period, predicted vs. realized if distinguishable).
Funding timestamp schedule (e.g., every 8h; per-venue exact cutoffs in UTC).
Borrow / margin constraints if the short leg requires borrow.
Liquidation rules: maintenance margin, liquidation engine model, partial-vs-full liquidation behaviour.
Basis calculation inputs: how mark/index are computed; documented index composition.
category_specific_validity: Funding rows without their settlement-period boundaries are invalid.
Rows are invalid if the perp's mark/index source changed mid-window without disclosure.
Borrow-cost rows are invalid if borrow availability cannot be confirmed for the period.

supported_arbitrage_categories.label: C. Triangular arbitrage
data_needed: Three synchronized pair quote streams (single venue) sharing one UTC clock at sub-second resolution.
Bid/ask for each leg with documented quote-update rate per pair.
Taker fees per leg (and maker fees per leg if the protocol's MVT permits maker fills).
Minimum order sizes per pair (notional and quantity).
Precision / rounding rules per pair (tick size, lot size).
Depth per leg at the test size.
Execution sequence assumptions: which leg first, expected slippage by the time leg N is sent.
category_specific_validity: A snapshot is invalid if any of the three legs is older than the documented max age (default 250 ms).
Snapshots are invalid if any leg's quote is missing or zero-bid/zero-ask.
Snapshots are invalid if displayed top-of-book size is below the configured min-fill on any leg.

supported_arbitrage_categories.label: D. Futures calendar / basis arbitrage
data_needed: Front-month and next-month contract quote series, with explicit roll dates and the chosen roll method (open-interest / volume / calendar).
Expiry calendar per contract (last trading day, settlement date, settlement method).
Contract multiplier and tick size per contract.
Roll schedule (which days the operator's protocol would have rolled).
Margin assumptions (initial + maintenance) per contract per venue.
Carry / funding / cost-of-capital assumptions used to compute fair basis.
category_specific_validity: Rows are invalid if the contract multiplier or tick size is missing.
Rows are invalid across a roll day if the roll method is not documented.
Rows are invalid if the expiry calendar disagrees with the cited venue source.

supported_arbitrage_categories.label: E. Statistical / relative-value mispricing (NOT pure arbitrage)
data_needed: Historical aligned price series for both legs (close-to-close OR intraday at the documented bar size).
Spread / z-score definitions to be used (log-ratio, beta-adjusted spread, distance-from-rolling-mean, etc.).
Correlation / stationarity / cointegration diagnostics over the IS window only.
Regime labels if regime gating is part of the strategy spec (e.g., HIGH_VOL / LOW_VOL).
Transaction cost assumptions (fees, spread, slippage, borrow on the short leg).
Explicit note in every downstream artifact: this category is RELATIVE_VALUE, not ARBITRAGE.
category_specific_validity: Rows are invalid if both legs are not aligned to the same bar timestamp.
Cointegration / correlation diagnostics computed on OOS data invalidate the study (look-ahead).
Rows are invalid during corporate-action / fork events without an explicit adjustment.

venue_requirements.named_per_test: Every category-level test must NAME the venue(s) in writing before any data acquisition is authorized.
venue_requirements.fee_schedule_attached: The contract requires a fee schedule for each named venue, including tier conditions if a tier higher than default is assumed.
venue_requirements.withdrawal_latency_assumption: Each venue carries a documented withdrawal/deposit latency assumption for slow-day conditions, not best-case.
venue_requirements.counterparty_haircut: Each venue carries a counterparty / custody haircut even if zero is the chosen value (so the assumption is explicit).
venue_requirements.no_live_connection: This contract authorizes NO live connection to any venue. Venues are inputs to the spec; the data-acquisition bundle is separate and separately authorized.
venue_requirements.regulatory_disclosure: Venue jurisdiction and applicable regulatory restrictions are part of the venue record.

instrument_requirements.instrument_id: Canonical per-venue symbol AND a SPARTA-side canonical name; never confuse aliases.
instrument_requirements.instrument_class: spot | perp | futures_dated | option_calendar | etf | other.
instrument_requirements.quote_currency: Document the quoting unit explicitly (USDT, USDC, USD, etc.).
instrument_requirements.corporate_action_log: For statistical RV on equities/ETFs, a corporate-action/dividend log is required.

accepted_timestamp_fields: local_recv_ts (always recorded; never the sole timestamp)
timestamp_requirements.exchange_vs_local_skew: Document per-venue clock skew between exchange_send_ts and local_recv_ts; report the rolling distribution, not just a single number.
timestamp_requirements.max_skew_allowed: Default 250 ms for cross-venue and triangular studies; tighter for studies that depend on top-of-book convergence.
timestamp_requirements.stale_quote_rule: A quote is stale if its exchange timestamp is older than (max_skew_allowed + venue_publish_interval).

alignment_rules.cross_venue_alignment: Align by exchange_send_ts when available; never align last-trade vs. mid-quote across venues.
alignment_rules.triangular_alignment: All three legs share the same source clock at sub-second resolution.
alignment_rules.spot_perp_alignment: Align spot to perp's mark/index timestamp; do NOT mix venues unless explicitly documented.

timestamp_requirements.timezone_normalization: All stored timestamps are UTC; any source in local time is converted at ingest and the original timezone preserved as metadata.
bar_aggregation_rules_if_used: Trade bars: documented bar size, time-bar vs. tick-bar vs. volume-bar; left-closed / right-open by default.
Quote bars: explicit choice (snapshot at bar close vs. last quote in window).
row_invalidity_criteria: exchange timestamp older than its publish interval + max_skew_allowed
exchange timestamp ahead of UTC wall clock (clock-skew anomaly)
duplicate primary timestamp without explicit sequence number
row spans a documented exchange outage without an outage flag

order_book_requirements.minimum_level: L1 (top-of-book) at minimum; L2 (5-10 levels) preferred for any depth-at-size test.
order_book_requirements.snapshot_or_delta: Either full snapshot or snapshot+delta with documented re-sync rule.
order_book_requirements.missing_side_rule: A row missing either side of L1 is invalid.
order_book_requirements.crossed_book_rule: A crossed top-of-book (bid >= ask) is flagged and excluded from MVT unless the strategy explicitly handles it.

trade_print_requirements.aggressor_side: Recorded if the venue exposes it; otherwise marked unknown (never inferred for arbitrage MVTs).
trade_print_requirements.block_trades_or_OTC_prints: Flagged separately from continuous book trades.

quote_requirements.min_quote_frequency: Document the venue's typical quote update interval per instrument; lower bound is the MVT clock granularity.
quote_requirements.quote_flicker_filter: Quote changes that revert within 1 venue tick are flagged but not dropped.
quote_requirements.quote_age_filter: Any quote older than max_skew_allowed at decision time is excluded.

fee_requirements.taker_fee_bps: Required per venue per instrument-class. Default sizing assumption is TAKER.
fee_requirements.fee_tier_assumption: Default = lowest standard tier the operator could realistically maintain; never best-case.
fee_requirements.network_fees: Recorded for crypto networks (gas / on-chain fee distribution).
fee_requirements.fee_as_pnl_line: Every backtest must carry fees as a distinct PnL line, never netted silently.

funding_requirements.history_horizon: At least one full funding cycle of warmup before any test bar; longer for stationarity checks.
funding_requirements.stress_event_label: Funding rows that fall within a documented stress event (e.g., crash-day) are labelled.
funding_requirements.borrow_cost_proxy: For short-leg pair trades, an explicit borrow-rate proxy is part of the contract; missing is treated as invalid.

withdrawal_deposit_requirements.expected_latency_distribution: P50 / P90 / P99 from operator records or vendor disclosure (slow-day biased).
withdrawal_deposit_requirements.outage_history: Withdrawal/deposit suspensions are first-class events in the data, not silent gaps.

liquidity_requirements.minimum_notional_depth_at_touch: Documented per category per venue per pair.
liquidity_requirements.depth_at_size_calculation: Cumulative size from best bid/ask up to the test size; never assume infinite liquidity at the quote.
liquidity_requirements.volume_filter: Pair-level minimum 24h volume threshold per category; below threshold = excluded from MVT.
liquidity_requirements.stale_order_book_filter: Any L2 snapshot older than max_skew_allowed at decision time is excluded.
liquidity_requirements.quote_flicker_filter: Recurring multi-tick flicker on a single side within a sub-second window is flagged.
liquidity_requirements.exchange_downtime_or_outage_flags: First-class boolean column; not inferred from gaps.

latency_requirements.publish_to_recv_latency_estimate_per_ven: Documented; used as additive haircut on top-of-book size we believe we could lift.
latency_requirements.decision_to_fill_latency: For any MVT that fires multi-leg, document the assumed time-to-fill on each leg.
latency_requirements.leg_sequence_risk_window: Slowest leg defines the risk window in cross-venue or triangular tests.

data_quality_rules.duplicates: Removed on (instrument_id, primary_ts, sequence_no_if_present); duplicates without a sequence number are invalid.
data_quality_rules.missing_values: Never silently forward-filled; gaps preserved with explicit gap flags.
data_quality_rules.out_of_order: Strictly monotonic by primary_ts; out-of-order rows are flagged.
data_quality_rules.outlier_handling: Outliers identified by venue/asset-specific rules (e.g., trade > X * rolling-median bid-ask spread); flagged not dropped.
data_quality_rules.reproducibility: Same raw inputs + same code + same contract version must produce bit-for-bit identical processed dataset.
data_quality_rules.provenance: Every dataset row carries a source identifier and a contract version; never anonymous.

normalization_rules.currency_normalization: Documented quote currency per pair; conversions across stablecoins/fiat require an explicit FX series.
normalization_rules.symbol_canonicalization: Per-venue symbol -> SPARTA canonical name; alias tables versioned alongside the dataset.
normalization_rules.tick_lot_rounding: Documented per instrument; never silently rounded.
normalization_rules.stitching_rules_for_continuous_contracts: Only the documented roll method; the roll convention is metadata, not folklore.
normalization_rules.split_dividend_adjustment_for_equities: If equities are used (statistical RV), the adjustment method is documented; raw + adjusted both stored.

future_allowed_data_sources: Existing on-disk frozen historical files already present in SPARTA's research tree (NO network).
A future, SEPARATELY authorized data-acquisition bundle (Protocol P2) which itself ships its own data-contract addendum and explicit authorization.
Operator-supplied paid-vendor exports that DO NOT require a live API connection from SPARTA itself (vendor pushes files; SPARTA reads them offline).
forbidden_data_sources_or_methods: Live exchange APIs from this contract's runtime.
Scraping any venue HTML / WebSocket / undocumented endpoint.
Any data source requiring an embedded API key, OAuth token, or .env credential.
Any data source that requires SPARTA to MAKE a network call from its runtime.
Any synthetic / mock-priced data used in place of real historical data.
Any dataset whose provenance cannot be cited line-by-line.
Any dataset that has been peeked at in its OOS window before sealing.
Any feed whose terms of service forbid research use.
required_future_artifacts: Per-category 'data acquisition memo' that names venue(s), pairs, fee tier, withdrawal-latency assumption, IS/OOS windows, and storage path before any data pull.
Per-category 'data integrity report' (P3 OUTPUT) showing row counts, gap stats, outage overlap, fee schedule attached, contract version pinned.
Per-category 'cost model snapshot' (maker, taker, spread, slippage, funding, borrow, withdrawal latency, counterparty haircut) sealed before any IS run.
Per-category stress overlay scenarios (>=1) with quantified PnL deduction.
Backtest report.json + report.md with PASS/WATCH/FAIL verdict against the Arbitrage Research Protocol v1 rules.
Lane closeout memo for any category that PARKs / RETIREs.

pass_watch_fail_rules.PASS: All required sections of this contract are present for the category under test; the data integrity report has zero unresolved flags; fee + funding + cost-of-capital lines are non-null and sourced; IS/OOS windows were sealed before any run.
pass_watch_fail_rules.WATCH: Contract is satisfied but at least one liquidity / latency / coverage assumption is borderline; the lane is documented and re-checked before any further phase.
pass_watch_fail_rules.FAIL: Any required section is missing, any forbidden data source was used, any safety flag is True, any OOS window was peeked, or any reproducibility check fails.

safety_boundaries: No broker control, no exchange connection, no API keys, no .env, no credential handling.
No live trading. No paper-order execution. No order placement.
No scheduler / background daemon. No external network calls in this contract's runtime.
No data fetch in this bundle. Data acquisition is a SEPARATE, explicitly authorized bundle (Protocol P2).
Do not modify paper / live execution files.
Do not claim profitability. Do not claim live-readiness. Do not claim STRONG evidence from this contract alone.
A price gap is not profit. Apparent edge != profit. Pure arbitrage is rare.
no_profit_claim_policy: A price gap is not profit.
Any future test must subtract all known costs (fees, spread, slippage, funding, borrow, withdrawal latency, counterparty haircut, capital lockup).
Any future test must include conservative (not best-case) slippage.
Any future result is invalid if it ignores fees, latency, liquidity, or funding.
This contract does not authorize trading.
This contract does not authorize an exchange connection.
This contract does not promote arbitrage to ACTIVE / STRONG by its existence.
